data.title: NATO seeks early Turkish F-16 deployment for Baltic air patrols
data.description:
NATO asked Turkey to deploy F-16 fighter jets to the Baltic Air Policing mission in 2026, advancing Ankara’s participation by several months, Bloomberg News reported Monday, citing people familiar with the discussions.

Under the proposal, Turkish aircraft would carry out patrols from Estonia for four months starting in August and running through December. The request reflects heightened alliance vigilance following repeated Russian airspace violations in the Baltic region.

Turkey had already been scheduled to contribute aircraft to NATO’s air policing effort in Romania from December 2026 through March 2027, the people said. Ankara has yet to decide whether it will accept the earlier deployment.

Turkish jets last flew Baltic air patrols in early 2025. The country’s defense ministry declined to comment on the latest request, while NATO did not immediately respond to inquiries.

The issue comes as the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan prepares to host the NATO leaders’ summit in July.

Security concerns have intensified for Turkey in recent months. In December, a Turkish F-16 shot down an unmanned aerial vehicle over the Black Sea as it neared Turkish airspace. Days later, a drone believed to be of Russian origin was discovered in northwestern Turkey, underscoring Ankara’s sensitivity to spillover risks from the conflict region.

data.title: Best and worst-rated stocks heading into earnings
data.description:
With the fourth quarter earnings season approaching, Seeking Alpha generated a list of best- and worst-rated mid- to mega-cap stocks as per its Quant model.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

In the week ahead, 14 S&P 500 companies—including two Dow 30 members—are set to report fourth-quarter earnings. FactSet estimates year-over-year earnings growth for the S&P 500 at 8.3% in Q4, which, if realized, would mark the index’s 10th straight quarter of earnings growth.

Here’s a look at the top-rated stocks as per Seeking Alpha’s quant model:

  • Micron Technology (MU), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • Lumentum Holdings (LITE), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • Nuvation Bio (NUVB), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • New Gold (NGD), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • Sibanye Stillwater (SBSW), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • Ciena (CIEN), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • Applied Digital (APLD), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • CommScope (COMM), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • nLIGHT (LASR), Quant Rating 4.97 – Strong Buy.
  • Lundin Mining (LUNMF), Quant Rating 4.97 – Strong Buy.

Here are the lowest-rated stocks as per Seeking Alpha’s quant model:

  • Fermi (FRMI), Quant Rating 1.00 – Strong Sell.
  • Ørsted (DNNGY), Quant Rating 1.03 – Strong Sell.
  • Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE), Quant Rating 1.05 – Strong Sell.
  • Lucid Group (LCID), Quant Rating 1.05 – Strong Sell.
  • Corcept Therapeutics (CORT), Quant Rating 1.06 – Strong Sell.
  • Trex (TREX), Quant Rating 1.09 – Strong Sell.
  • Telix Pharmaceuticals (TLX), Quant Rating 1.10 – Strong Sell.
  • Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR), Quant Rating 1.11 – Strong Sell.
  • BellRing Brands (BRBR), Quant Rating 1.13 – Strong Sell.
  • Zai Lab (ZLAB), Quant Rating 1.13 – Strong Sell.

Looking at sectors, Industrials (XLI), Technology (XLK), and Materials (XLB) were the highest-rated (“Buy”) among the 11 S&P 500 (SP500) sectors, with a Quant score of 4.12, 4.10, and 4.03, respectively. Utilities (XLU) was rated Hold, while Consumer Staples (XLP) and Real Estate (XLRE) received Strong Sell ratings.

As per FactSet, eight of the 11 S&P 500 sectors are projected to report year-over-year growth, led by the information technology sector. On the other hand, three sectors are predicted to report a year-over-year decline in earnings, led by the Consumer Discretionary sector.

Here is a list of ETFs to track the broader U.S. market: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

data.title: Activist Engaged Capital plans proxy fight at BlackLine - Reuters
data.description:
Activist investor Engaged Capital plans to start a proxy fight to install four directors at software maker BlackLine (BL) as it works to pressure the company to pursue a possible sale. BlackLine rose 1.5%. 

While BlackLine (BL) has had some contact with Engaged, the hedge fund isn't satisfied and is ready to start a proxy fight, according to a Reuters report on Monday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

The nominees include Christopher Hetrick, Engaged's director of research; Christopher Young; Christopher Hallenbeck; and Storm Duncan, according to the report. 

The latest Engaged move comes after the activist in October pushed the company to pursue a sale after a Bloomberg report that SAP (SAP) made an offer for Blackline in the high $60s/share last year, a bid that was rejected by the company.

Bloomberg also reported in November that several investors in accounting software company BlackLine (BL) were pressuring the firm to explore a sale after reports of interest from SAP (SAP).

data.title: Capital One to provide $425M to customers in new settlement over 360 Savings accounts
data.description:
New York Attorney General Letitia James commended a new settlement requiring Capital One Financial (COF) to provide $425M in restitution and better interest rates for its 360 Savings customers, she said on Monday. 

The AG, along with a number of other states, had accused the bank of misleading customers by marketing its 360 Savings accounts as "high interest" accounts, but instead its "360 Performance Savings" provided much higher rates.

The original proposed settlement would have provided less than $300M in restitution payments and would allow Capital One (COF) to continue paying the lower interest rate on 360 Savings accounts, the AG said.

The new settlement, which was preliminarily approved by the court today, will require Capital One to pay $425M in restitution, including an estimated $34M to New Yorkers who had 360 Savings accounts, the NY AG office said. In addition, the bank is required to match 360 Savings and 360 Performance Savings interest rates, erasing the misleading two-tiered system of accounts.

The Office of the Attorney General will voluntarily dismiss its lawsuit if the revised class action settlement receives final approval from the court and goes into effect, the office said.

In January 2025, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau sued Capital One (COF), for allegedly misleading consumers about the interest rates on its 360 Savings account. The agency later dropped several suits against the bank.

Capital One (COF) stock fell 6.4% in Monday afternoon trading after President Trump ordered a 10% credit card interest rate cap.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wall Street pushes higher despite Powell investigation
data.description:
Wall Street trades higher on Monday despite reports that the U.S. attorney’s office has opened a criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was up by 0.4%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was near even. 

From a sector vantage point, six of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors were in negative trading territory with Financials suffering the most.  At the other end of the spectrum, Consumer Staples have been the strongest performer on the session.

The investigation reportedly centers on the central bank’s renovation of its Washington headquarters and whether Powell lied to Congress about the project’s scope, according to the New York Times. 

Powell released an extraordinary statement, asserting that grand jury subpoenas served by the Department of Justice are not about legitimate legal concerns but rather punishment for the central bank’s refusal to set interest rates according to President Donald Trump’s preferences. 

U.S. Treasury yields inched higher across the curve. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 1 basis point to 3.54%, while the U.S.10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 1 basis point to 4.17%. 

The economic calendar is light, with investors eyeing inflation data later this week in the form of the consumer price index and the producer price index. 

“We expect Tuesday’s CPI report to show inflation holding steady in the 2.7% range. While we may see a slight 'pop' in monthly goods prices due to ongoing tariff pass-throughs, the broader trend is clearly drifting toward our 2026 target of 2.7%–2.9%,” Robert Edwards, chief investment officer of Edwards Asset Management said. 

“2026 is likely to be a sawtooth year for the markets, where stocks experience a 7-15% pullback in the first half of the year for the simple reason that too much of Wall Street is bullish. The market is due for a sentiment reset and we expect that reset to take place in the next 6-months. This sentiment reset is necessary fuel for the next leg of the bull market. We expect stocks to reach record highs by year-end,” Edwards added.

data.title: Crypto funds record outflows of $454M last week - report
data.description:
Cryptocurrency investment products reported outflows of $454M last week, as a four-day streak of outflows totaling $1.3B almost completely reversed the $1.5B in inflows recorded in the first two days of the year. 

James Butterfill, CoinShares’ Head of Research, said this turnaround in sentiment appears to stem mainly from investor worries over the diminishing prospects of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut in March following recent macro data releases.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) ETFs recorded outflows of $405M last week, according to a CoinShare report. However, short-Bitcoin products saw $9.2M in outflows, sending mixed signals about overall market sentiment toward the asset.

Ethereum (ETH-USD) posted outflows of $116M last week, while multi-asset products recorded outflows of $21M.

In contrast, XRP and Solana (SOL-USD) recorded the inflows last week, amounting to $45.8M and $32.8M, respectively, as positive sentiment towards the tokens persisted.

The U.S. saw outflows totaling $569M last week. On the other hand, Germany led with inflows of $58.9M, followed by Canada at $24.5M and Switzerland at $21M.

data.title: These mid-cap financials lag as EPS revisions signal softer momentum ahead of earnings
data.description:
As earnings season nears, Seeking Alpha highlights the mid-cap financial stocks showing the weakest EPS revisions. Each company on this list has received a D+ EPS revisions grade, signaling softer momentum in analyst earnings expectations heading into the reporting period.

At the lower end of the spectrum from a Quant Rating perspective are PagSeguro Digital Ltd., Inter & Co, Inc. (INTR), and Golub Capital BDC (GBDC). 

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings system grades stocks on their relative performance across various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any rating of 2.5 or below considered a bearish rating.

Here is the list:

  • PagSeguro Digital Ltd. (PAGS), Quant Rating of 3.23.
  • Inter & Co, Inc. (INTR), Quant Rating of 3.18.
  • Golub Capital BDC (GBDC), Quant Rating of 3.03.
  • Atlantic Union Bankshares Corporation (AUB), Quant Rating of 2.94.
  • WaFd, Inc. (WAFD), Quant Rating of 2.83.
  • NCR Atleos Corporation (NATL), Quant Rating of 2.82.
  • BOK Financial Corporation (BOKF), Quant Rating of 2.86. 
  • Independent Bank Corp. (INDB), Quant Rating of 2.85. 
  • Selective Insurance Group, Inc. (SIGI), Quant Rating of 2.42.

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO).

data.title: Paramount files lawsuit against Warner Bros; intends to nominate director slate (update)
data.description:
Update 1:20pm: Adds Warner Bros. response.

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) plans to nominate directors to Warner Bros. Discovery 's (WBD)  board as it continues its pursuit of the entertainment giant. Warner Bros. fell 1.9%.

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) also filed a lawsuit in Delaware court seeking to gain basic information to enable Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) shareholders to make an informed decision, according to a  letter sent to Warner Bros. holders on Monday. 

Paramount (PSKY) plans to propose an amendment to Warner Bros.'s bylaws that will require WBD shareholder approval for any separation of Global Networks. 

"We do not undertake any of these actions lightly," the letter said. "Make no mistake, our goal remains to have constructive discussions with WBD's Board to reach an agreement that is in the best interests of WBD shareholders." 

Paramount (PSKY) has said that its $30 a share all-cash offer for Warner Bros. is superior to the Netflix (NFLX) cash and stock deal and that it carries fewer risks.

Warner Bros. (WBD) responded to the Paramount (PSKY) lawsuit, calling it "meritless" and is "seeking to distract," according to an emailed statement to Bloomberg. 

“Despite six weeks and just as many press releases from Paramount Skydance, it has yet to raise the price or address the numerous and obvious deficiencies of its offer,” Warner Bros. said in an emailed statement to Bloomberg.

Under the terms of the agreement announced December 5, Netflix (NFLX) will acquire Warner Bros. (WBD), including its film and television studios, HBO Max, and HBO, in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at $27.75 per WBD share, with a total enterprise value of approximately $82.7 billion (equity value of $72.0 billion).

data.title: Axalta downgraded at UBS as Akzo deal tempers near-term upside
data.description:
UBS on Monday downgraded Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) to Neutral from Buy, citing a more balanced risk-reward profile following the proposed acquisition by Akzo Nobel (AKZOF) (AKZOY) and limited near-term catalysts for the stock.

In a note dated Jan. 12, UBS analyst Joshua Spector lowered his price target on Axalta shares to $35 from $39, arguing that much of the takeover optimism is already reflected in the stock after a roughly 15% rally since the deal was announced.

Deal logic intact but benefits pushed out

UBS said the strategic rationale for combining Axalta (AXTA) and Akzo remains sound, particularly given the potential for cost savings that could support earnings growth for both companies. However, those synergies are unlikely to materialize for several years, with meaningful benefits not expected until closer to 2028.

While UBS estimates the combined company could eventually unlock more than $600 million in annual cost savings, the firm said little of that upside is currently being priced in by investors. The pro forma valuation implies only modest upside for Axalta shares in a deal-completion scenario, according to the report.

Stand-alone outlook remains muted

Outside the transaction, UBS sees limited organic growth prospects for Axalta in the medium term. The firm expects only low single-digit earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization growth through 2026 and does not forecast a full recovery in the company’s Refinish coatings business until the second half of that year.

UBS warned that if investors begin to view Axalta (AXTA) as a no-growth story, the stock could trade at a lower valuation multiple, potentially pressuring shares despite the company’s improving free cash flow profile

Cash builds but deployment uncertain

Axalta (AXTA) is expected to generate substantial free cash flow over the next two years, but UBS noted that capital deployment is effectively on hold while the company awaits clarity on the Akzo transaction. In a deal-break scenario, UBS said Axalta (AXTA) would have flexibility to deploy cash, but investor confidence could weaken until management outlines a longer-term strategy.

Given these factors, UBS said the stock now offers limited upside relative to downside risk, leading to the downgrade to Neutral.
